risks.description: The tooth tip rounding value for the external helical gear is unclear, so the gear profile definition is not fully specified for final manufacture/inspection.
risks.mitigation: Obtain clarification or approved gear data before release; manufacture remains physically feasible with JAL's gear hobbing/grinding/honing capability once defined.

risks.description: Case hardening to 630+100 HV10 with 0.3+0.2 mm case depth can distort the small gear and affect the Ø9 H7 bore and gear runout/composite errors.
risks.mitigation: Use controlled in-house sealed-quench heat treatment and final precision finishing/inspection after heat treatment where required.

risks.description: The Rz 2.5 callout location is uncertain between the bore and adjacent face.
risks.mitigation: Confirm drawing intent; if applied to the bore, JAL's bore honing and surface-finish inspection can achieve it.

risks.description: The 23.3 ±0.017 mm axis-distance-with-counter-gear criterion depends on a correct master/counter gear or inspection fixture.
risks.mitigation: Use certified gear measurement equipment or approved master gear during inspection.

feasibility.assessment_notes: The part is a small Ø39.79 x 14 mm case-hardened left-hand helical gear with a Ø9 H7 through bore. JAL has in-house alloy-steel machining, gear hobbing/shaping/shaving/grinding/honing, bore honing/ID grinding, heat treatment, finishing, and inspection capabilities, and the part is well within machine envelopes.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Cylindrical gear body
spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: No explicit plus/minus; general DIN ISO 2768-mK applies unless otherwise specified
spec_assessments.reasoning: A 14 mm axial width on a Ø39.79 mm steel gear body is within CNC lathe/VMC and grinding capability.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Cylindrical gear body
spec_assessments.reasoning: A 0.05 mm unilateral OD tolerance is within CNC turning normal capability and easily within OD grinding capability.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Cylindrical gear body
spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Case-hardened and tempered per heat-treatment note
spec_assessments.reasoning: JAL has sealed-quench case-carburizing furnaces covering 0.3-0.8 mm case depth, but heat-treatment distortion can affect the finished gear body.
spec_assessments.risk_description: Distortion after case hardening may affect OD, faces, bore coaxiality, and runout-related features.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Use controlled in-house heat treatment and finish critical surfaces after heat treat if required.

feature_assessments.notes: The basic cylindrical body is manufacturable; the risk is tied to maintaining final precision after case hardening.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Central through bore Ø9 H7
spec_assessments.reasoning: A Ø9 H7 precision through bore is within JAL's ID grinding and bore honing capabilities.
spec_assessments.required_process: reaming plus bore honing or ID grinding

spec_assessments.feature_name: Central through bore Ø9 H7
spec_assessments.spec_description: Through bore depth equal to part width
spec_assessments.reasoning: A 14 mm through bore at Ø9 mm is shallow and accessible axially from both faces.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: cnc_lathe drilling_on_center/boring; vmc drilling/reaming; bore_honing available

spec_assessments.feature_name: Central through bore Ø9 H7
spec_assessments.spec_description: Local surface texture possibly on bore
spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Rz 2.5 if leader applies to bore
spec_assessments.reasoning: Bore honing can achieve fine bore finish, but the feature graph notes the callout location is moderately uncertain.
spec_assessments.risk_description: If the callout applies to a different surface, process/inspection focus changes.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Confirm whether Rz 2.5 applies to the Ø9 H7 bore; if yes, use bore honing and surface-finish measurement.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Central through bore Ø9 H7
spec_assessments.spec_description: Bore tolerance retained on case-hardened part
spec_assessments.nominal_value: Case-hardened part with Ø9 H7 bore
spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Bore remains Ø9 H7 after heat treatment
spec_assessments.reasoning: The shop can heat treat and finish hard bores, but maintaining H7 after case hardening requires distortion control or finish honing/grinding after heat treatment.
spec_assessments.risk_description: Heat-treatment distortion could move the bore outside H7 if not finish processed after hardening.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Control heat treat and perform final bore honing/ID grinding if required by final condition.
spec_assessments.required_process: heat treat plus bore honing/ID grinding

feature_assessments.notes: The bore is feasible with in-house precision bore finishing; heat-treatment distortion and the uncertain Rz 2.5 leader are the main risks.

spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: Serves as datum A for 0.035 runout control
spec_assessments.reasoning: Facing, grinding, and lapping capabilities can produce a stable planar datum face for inspection.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: cnc_lathe facing; od_grinding surface_grinding; lapping face_lapping

spec_assessments.mitigation: Finish grind/lap the datum face after heat treatment if required.
spec_assessments.required_process: heat treat and possible face grinding/lapping

feature_assessments.notes: Datum face creation is feasible; risk is related to post-heat-treatment datum stability.

feature_assessments.notes: The specified OD edge chamfers are routine and feasible.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Central bore entry chamfers 2X 0.7 at 30°
spec_assessments.spec_description: Bore entry chamfer size or land
spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: No explicit plus/minus; exact interpretation somewhat ambiguous
spec_assessments.reasoning: The geometry is easy to machine, but the feature graph notes ambiguity in whether 0.7 is chamfer size or axial land.
spec_assessments.risk_description: Incorrect interpretation could produce a nonconforming bore-mouth detail.
spec_assessments.mitigation: Confirm the 0.7 mm definition; then machine with standard axial chamfering capability.

spec_assessments.feature_name: Central bore entry chamfers 2X 0.7 at 30°
spec_assessments.tolerance_or_requirement: No explicit plus/minus; general angular tolerance applies
spec_assessments.reasoning: A 30° chamfer, equivalent to a 60° included cone in the feature graph, is machinable with standard chamfer/countersink tooling.

feature_assessments.notes: The bore-mouth chamfers are physically feasible, but the 0.7 mm interpretation should be confirmed.

spec_assessments.feature_name: General deburred/unspecified edge break condition
spec_assessments.reasoning: General 0.3 mm edge breaking and deburring are supported by in-house bench finishing and chamfering capability.
spec_assessments.shop_capability_reference: finish_bench deburr and edge_break; cnc_lathe/vmc chamfering

spec_assessments.feature_name: General deburred/unspecified edge break condition
spec_assessments.reasoning: A general R0.4 edge condition is feasible with manual edge break/tumbling/finishing processes.

feature_assessments.notes: General edge deburring is fully within in-house capability.

clarifications.question: What is the required tooth tip rounding value for the gear tooth form?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The exact tooth tip/root rounding affects the finished gear profile and inspection criteria.

clarifications.question: Does the local Rz 2.5 surface texture callout apply to the Ø9 H7 bore or to the adjacent axial datum face?
clarifications.why_it_matters: It determines which surface requires the fine finish process and inspection.
clarifications.suggested_default: Apply Rz 2.5 to the Ø9 H7 bore.

clarifications.question: Do the 0.7 and 30° callouts describe matching chamfers at both mouths of the Ø9 H7 bore?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The bore entry geometry is visible but the exact meaning of the 0.7 dimension is ambiguous.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as 2X bore entry chamfers, 0.7 mm at 30°.

clarifications.question: Please confirm the unilateral tolerance on the outside/head diameter is Ø39.79 +0/-0.05 mm.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The OD/head diameter tolerance is critical to the body and gear envelope.

assumptions.text: 16MnCrS5 1.7139 is treated as an alloy/case-hardening steel within the shop's steel material capability.
The Ø9 H7 bore is interpreted using the standard H7 tolerance class for this diameter range and is assessed against honing/ID grinding capability.
Rz finish values are assessed qualitatively against grinding/honing/surface-finish capabilities without converting Rz to Ra.
No outside processing is permitted or needed because the shop profile lists all required gear manufacturing, heat treatment, grinding/honing, finishing, and inspection processes in-house.

reasoning_summary: This is a small case-hardened external helical gear with a precision Ø9 H7 through bore, and it fits easily within JAL's turning, VMC, gear, grinding, honing, heat-treatment, and inspection envelopes. The shop can physically manufacture all required feature types in-house, including gear cutting/finishing, bore honing/ID grinding, and case hardening. The assessment is feasible with risks because the tooth tip rounding is unclear and heat-treatment distortion plus uncertain Rz 2.5 callout placement require clarification/control before production release.
